name: RaidCarry
description: A consumer-focused service marketplace offering paid raid carries and coaching (primarily for WoW) targeting players who want to skip progression time or secure raid loot/achievements.

details.targetMarket: MMO players (casual and time-poor) who want guaranteed raid clears, loot, achievements or one-on-one coaching rather than investing the time in guild progression.

items: Scheduled raid clears / full raid carries (multiple difficulties)
Discord-based support / scheduling (public Discord invite advertised)
Refund/guarantee language on successful completion (common in market but varies by provider)

missingFeatures: Transparent, itemized pricing and standardized packages (no publicly visible per-run price catalog found) — opportunity to win by offering clear, comparable packages and per-difficulty price tables
Verified booster identities and skill verification (proof of guild history, logs, or K/D‑style stats) — current market relies on reputation/Discord which is weak for high-ticket purchases
Escrow and stronger buyer protections (in-platform escrow, staged payments for multi-boss runs) — many sellers use up-front payments with limited standardized protections
Analytics and progress tracking for coaching buyers (session notes, replay links, performance metrics) — most services focus on one-off runs, not measurable improvement
B2B/guild tooling or APIs to let guilds book multiple slots or onboard teams — current offerings are consumer-facing and ad-hoc

pricingPlans.name: Custom / per-order
pricingPlans.note: No public, standardized pricing found on the primary site; pricing appears to be quoted per order or via Discord.

pricingEvidence.label: RaidCarry site (primary URL)
pricingEvidence.claim: Primary company site; when programmatically fetched it was unreachable (no public price catalog discovered).

pricingEvidence.label: Alternate domain / product page
pricingEvidence.claim: Public-facing product copy advertising WoW raid and Mythic+ carry services and a Discord invite; no visible standardized price list on landing copy. (site may block automated requests.)

companyEvidence.label: Raidcarry service explainer (industry coverage)
companyEvidence.claim: Explains what raid-carry services are and references raidcarry-style services as a category; useful background when primary company details are unavailable.

companyEvidence.label: Primary URL (unreachable by automated fetch)
companyEvidence.claim: Company domain and entry point for customers (site returned fetch errors during research, preventing deeper automated scraping of company metadata).

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit discussion — buyer safety for raid carries
reviewEvidence.claim: Community discussion pointing to trust and safety concerns when paying for raid-carry services; supports claims about buyer trust risk and need for verification/escrow.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit discussion — is buying a carry normal
reviewEvidence.claim: Community sentiment and safety concerns around buying carries and potential for scams or poor experience, backing observed market weaknesses.

featureEvidence.label: RaidCarry alternative page (product copy)
featureEvidence.claim: Advertises WoW raid clears, Mythic+ carries and a public Discord link — supports that the product offers scheduled raid carries and Discord-based coordination.

featureEvidence.label: Overgear — example of typical raid-carry feature set
featureEvidence.claim: Shows common features in the market: scheduled raid carries, multiple difficulty levels, guaranteed clears and loot options — useful to infer feature parity/opportunity areas.

featureEvidence.label: Skycoach — scheduling, 24/7 availability claim
featureEvidence.claim: Example of 24/7 scheduling, guarantee language and multiple run types that are common in the raid-carry market.

strengths: Niche focus on raid carries and coaching — addresses a clear, time-sensitive customer need (fast progression, loot, achievements).
Simple buyer flow advertised (Discord-based scheduling) which lowers friction to book runs compared with multi-step marketplaces.
Covers both raid clears and single-boss/targeted carry products (based on product copy), allowing upsell/cross-sell across run types.
weaknesses: Opaque pricing and packaging — no public standardized price catalog was found, which raises buyer friction and comparison difficulty.
Trust and safety gaps: no public booster verification, scarce company transparency, and community warnings about scams in this market.
Site reliability / discoverability issues — primary domain returned fetch errors during research and there is little independent coverage, hurting organic acquisition and credibility.
Limited productized features for retention: no evidence of subscriptions, recurring bookings, coaching analytics, or developer APIs to support B2B/guild integrations.
